steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context (fill-in-blank) question. The blank completes the claim that "myoglobin deficiency in teleosts could be ______ habitat." The passage says pale hearts were once thought to be related to polar habitats, but tropical species also have pale hearts — meaning habitat does not determine myoglobin deficiency. Substitute each choice into the blank and check which one fits that meaning.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'irrespective of': "Irrespective of" means "regardless of" — myoglobin deficiency occurs regardless of habitat. This matches exactly: both polar and tropical species have pale hearts, so habitat does not control the trait. ✓
  • Choice B — 'mediated by': "Mediated by habitat" means habitat controls or transmits the deficiency. That is the old assumption the new discovery overturns — the opposite of what the evidence supports. ✗
  • Choice C — 'predictive of': "Predictive of habitat" reverses the relationship, suggesting deficiency predicts where a fish lives. The passage is about whether habitat predicts deficiency, not the reverse, and neither direction is supported by the evidence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'obscured by': "Obscured by habitat" means habitat hides the deficiency. The passage makes no claim that habitat conceals anything — the deficiency is visible in both environments. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
